expectations.text: Uses shadow-xl (ADS overlay elevation) — not shadow-md or shadow-lg
expectations.evidence: Agent deliberately used shadow-md (elevation.shadow.raised), not shadow-xl. Reasoning in summary Section 6: 'The skill's Overlay Elevation Shadow rule explicitly lists: dropdown-menu, popover, context-menu, menubar, combobox, select, hover-card. Tooltip is NOT in this list.' This is arguably a more nuanced, correct decision — ADS tooltip uses raised elevation, not overlay elevation.

expectations.text: No prop names, variant values, or sub-component names changed from original
expectations.evidence: Summary Section 5 confirms: 'All four sub-components keep their shadcn names: Tooltip, TooltipTrigger, TooltipContent, TooltipProvider. All prop names preserved: side, sideOffset, align, alignOffset, className, children, delay. No variants exist on this component, so none were added or renamed.'

expectations.text: Uses ADS tooltip background token (bg-bg-inverse-subtle or similar) — not generic bg-black or bg-popover
expectations.evidence: Uses bg-bg-neutral-bold which maps to ADS color.background.neutral.bold (#292A2E). Summary Section 1 confirms: 'Background | color.background.neutral.bold | #292A2E'. Browser verification in Section 7 confirms backgroundColor rgb(41, 42, 46) matches.

expectations.text: Does not add ring-1 border on the tooltip popup (ADS overlays use shadow only)
expectations.evidence: Modified tooltip.tsx className string in Section 4 shows no ring-* classes on the Popup element. Only shadow-md and outline-hidden are added.

expectations.text: pnpm run lint reports no new errors on modified files
expectations.evidence: Summary Section 7: 'pnpm exec eslint components/ui/tooltip.tsx --max-warnings=0 — (no output — 0 errors, 0 warnings). Result: PASS'

expectations.text: pnpm tsc --noEmit reports no new errors
expectations.evidence: Summary Section 7: 'No errors in components/ui/tooltip.tsx or any tooltip-related files. Result: PASS (0 new errors introduced)'

suggestions.reason: This assertion may be WRONG. The skill explicitly categorizes tooltip as a 'raised' element (shadow-md), NOT an 'overlay' element (shadow-xl). The agent's shadow-md choice is arguably more correct than shadow-xl. Consider revising the assertion to 'uses shadow-md (raised elevation) for tooltip' or making it test that the agent applies the correct shadow level per the component type.

No assertion checks for removal of stale Radix selectors (data-[state=delayed-open]:*). The agent correctly identified and removed these as Base UI uses data-open: instead. This is a quality improvement that goes unchecked.

eval_feedback.overall: The shadow assertion penalizes correct behavior. The agent made a sophisticated, well-reasoned decision to use shadow-md based on the skill's component categorization. Recommend revising the assertion.
